items.title: Ensure JSON payload is correctly forwarded for alias-rewrite in Platform Service ⚡️
items.summary: This update fixes the `/openai/images/edits` endpoint to properly set `Content-Type: application/json` when a model alias rewrite occurs. The previous implementation relied on a state flag, which failed because headers were sent before the body. The fix computes the alias condition inline for both headers and body, ensuring consistent behavior. Existing endpoints for gpt-image-2 generations and chat handlers remain unchanged.

items.title: Switching to Xiaojingai's GPT-Image-2 for Reliability in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces the unreliable upstream `gpt-image-2` route with Xiaojingai's `gpt-image-2-reverse`, ensuring consistent performance for image generation and editing requests. The public API remains unchanged, allowing seamless user experience while the backend now utilizes a more reliable model. Key changes include updates to configuration files and handler logic to support the new model aliasing mechanism.

items.title: Align Worker name in Wrangler config to deployed version in Frontend Web ⚡
items.summary: This update modifies the `wrangler.jsonc` configuration file to ensure the Worker name matches the deployed Worker in Cloudflare. The change corrects a naming mismatch, preventing potential deployment inconsistencies. Only a single line in the configuration was updated, keeping other settings intact. This helps maintain smoother CI/CD workflows for the frontend environment.

items.title: Survey Dialog Fixes and Improvements in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses several critical issues in the survey dialog, including fixing the single-select binding, correcting broken translations, and enhancing the layout for better readability. Additionally, the community group question has been removed based on user feedback, streamlining the survey process. The changes ensure a smoother user experience and improved accessibility for all users.

items.title: Fix admin announcement handling for consistent translations in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update ensures that the admin announcement page correctly uses the Chinese language for editing, preventing any overwriting of translations. Key changes include forcing the 'Accept-Language' header to 'zh-CN' for admin endpoints and modifying the translation submission process. These adjustments will maintain the integrity of translations across multiple languages.

items.title: Update Vue to 3.5.34 with key bug fixes in Frontend Web ⚡
items.summary: This PR upgrades Vue from version 3.5.33 to 3.5.34. The update includes several bug fixes in compiler-sfc, reactivity, runtime-core, and suspense, addressing issues like orphan effects, DOM leaks, and symbol coercion during props validation. Dependencies in package.json and package-lock.json were updated to reflect this change, ensuring smoother framework behavior and improved stability.

items.title: Update ESLint Plugin for Prettier in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the eslint-plugin-prettier from version 4.2.5 to 5.5.5, enhancing compatibility and fixing several issues. Key changes include updates to dependencies and improvements in rule options handling, which will help maintain code quality and consistency across the project.

items.title: Fix translation logic for announcements in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a critical issue where the translation for announcements was incorrectly using client-posted text instead of the original Chinese content. The backend now strictly utilizes the `translation_key` to fetch the correct Chinese source for translations, ensuring accuracy and consistency. Additionally, the frontend has been updated to streamline the translation process by removing unnecessary fields and enforcing proper locale handling.

items.title: Streamline User Onboarding with Silent Application Grants in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update replaces the modal dialog for new users with a silent auto-grant of global applications, enhancing the onboarding experience. Users will now receive a friendly toast welcoming them and informing them of their free credits upon their first visit. Existing users will also see this toast once per browser session after the update, ensuring everyone feels welcomed.

items.title: Automated Solana Token Research Published in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: The latest automated Solana token research report has been published, reviewing 18 candidates. Key findings include three tokens recommended to hold, while nine were skipped due to low scores and liquidity concerns. Notably, USMC had the highest pipeline score but was flagged as suspicious, leading to its exclusion.

items.title: Enhance WeChat Announcement Broadcasting in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new integration for broadcasting announcements to WeChat customer groups via the Wisdom service. When an admin publishes an announcement, the system now automatically sends the title and content to specified WeChat groups, ensuring that the messages are delivered efficiently and logged for any errors. Key changes include new utility functions for sending messages, updates to the announcement views, and additional environment settings for configuration.

items.title: Enhance Image Generation with OpenAI Compatibility in Platform Service 🎨
items.summary: This update introduces OpenAI-compatible image generation and editing endpoints backed by ephone's new `gpt-image-2` async task API. Key changes include the addition of `/images/generations` and `/images/edits` routes, which allow for seamless integration with existing OpenAI workflows. This enhancement provides a second upstream provider for image processing, enabling better load balancing and improved service reliability.

items.title: Enhance Suno API with New Audio Features in M C Ps 🎶
items.summary: This update introduces two new parameters to the Suno MCP server, aligning it with the upstream OpenAPI spec. The `audio_weight` parameter allows for advanced control over audio influence in cover operations, while the `lyric_prompt` enables auto-generation of lyrics when the lyric field is empty. These enhancements improve the flexibility and usability of the audio generation tools.

items.title: Hide Google connector skills for DeepSeek models in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This PR updates the `aichat2` worker to prevent DeepSeek models from accessing Google connector skills. A new policy file defines denied connectors per model group, and the skill filtering is applied in-place during request processing. Unit tests cover all edge cases, and the full worker test suite passes with no issues. This change ensures DeepSeek models no longer attempt unreliable multi-step OAuth flows, improving system reliability.

items.title: Clarify 30-day retention for mirrored WAV URLs in Backend 🎵
items.summary: Updated the Suno WAV integration docs to specify that mirrored WAV files on our CDN are retained for 30 days. The change only affects the Chinese documentation; other languages will update via the translation CronJob. No service code was modified, as the retention is handled by the COS bucket lifecycle policy. Users are advised to download files if they need them beyond the 30-day window.

items.title: Enhance sync functionality and optimize writes in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces critical improvements to the sync process in `sync.py`, addressing two main issues. The `--force` flag now functions correctly, significantly reducing unnecessary database writes from 7,500 to approximately 315 per deploy. Additionally, the order of operations has been adjusted to prioritize syncing documentation, ensuring that user-visible content is updated even in case of partial runs.

items.title: Ensure full hourly sync and immediate post-deploy updates in Backend ⚡
items.summary: This PR updates the platform-backend-sync CronJob to run hourly instead of every 5 minutes and prevents overlapping jobs by setting concurrencyPolicy to Forbid. Resource limits are increased to allow completion of large syncs, including ~4,400 doc entries. Additionally, deploy/run.sh now triggers an immediate post-deploy job, ensuring PR edits land in the database without waiting for the next scheduled run.

items.title: Correct mountsea channel usage in Platform Service 🔧
items.summary: Updated the mountsea worker to record `channel: 'sea'` instead of `'cortex'` in all api-usages payloads. This ensures the worker is correctly distinguished in CLS from other workers like ai318 and openaihk. The change affects three locations in the code: `response`, `request.json`, and `payload`. Build and type checks passed cleanly.

items.title: Normalize Empty Aspect Ratio and Resolution in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a critical issue where 96% of `/nano-banana/images` requests were returning HTTP 400 errors due to empty aspect ratio and resolution values. By normalizing these values to undefined, the system now allows for default fallbacks, improving the success rate of image generation requests. The fix has been applied across all relevant workers to ensure consistent behavior.

items.title: Update Suno WAV Documentation for CDN Mirroring in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the documentation for the Suno WAV API, reflecting the new persistent CDN mirror URL. Key changes include replacing outdated examples and clarifying the mirroring process to prevent 403 errors after upstream purges. The OpenAPI specification has also been updated to align with the new URL pattern, ensuring users have accurate information.

items.title: Fix survey module loading issue for Python 3.8 in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a 500 Internal Server Error occurring for authenticated users when accessing the survey endpoint. The root cause was the use of PEP 585 generic syntax in a module that was being loaded in a Python 3.8 environment. By adding a future import for annotations, the code is now compatible with Python 3.8 while maintaining functionality for Python 3.9 and above.

items.title: Weekly research update highlights TROLL hold and multiple skips in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: The 2026-05-06 research cycle added one report covering 18 candidates from watchlist and trending tokens. Grok probes were degraded, resulting in unknown credibility for all checked tokens. No candidates met the buy threshold, while TROLL was marked as a hold due to solid distribution and liquidity. Several tokens, including BEER, GME, and Goblin, were hard-skipped due to rug risks, high concentration, or cooling pumps.

items.title: Enhance WAV File Handling Across All Providers in Platform Service 🎉
items.summary: This update reopens a previous PR to extend the mirroring of WAV files to all four Suno providers: nebula, ttapi, openaihk, and suandao. It corrects the path for ttapi and implements a new helper function to ensure WAV files are stored in COS, preventing 403 errors from the upstream CDN. The changes improve the reliability of WAV file access for users.

items.title: Fix lyric handling in Platform Service 🎶
items.summary: This update addresses a bug where user-provided lyrics were silently dropped when submitting a cover request without the custom flag. The fix introduces an effective custom flag that ensures lyrics are preserved when provided, enhancing user experience. The changes were made across multiple audio handler files to ensure consistent behavior.

items.title: Enhance Suno Docs for Replace Section Clarity in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the documentation for the Suno `replace_section` feature by clarifying that it only returns the infill segment and requires a follow-up `concat` call to obtain the full song. The examples have been rewritten to reflect actual test results, ensuring users understand the workflow. Changes were made to both English and Chinese documentation files.

items.title: Trim Whitespace from App ID and User ID Inputs in Frontend Web ✨
items.summary: This update addresses an issue where leading or trailing spaces in App ID and User ID inputs caused queries to return no results. The inputs are now trimmed before being sent to the backend in various actions, ensuring accurate UUID string matches. The form will also reflect the trimmed values, enhancing user experience.

items.title: New Research Cycle Insights in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: The latest research cycle for May 6, 2026, has been completed, reviewing 19 candidates from various sources. Key findings include one hold recommendation for the BULL token, while 18 candidates were skipped due to low liquidity or suspicious activity. The market shows mild positivity with a 2.6% increase in SOL.
